spec_match.rationale: Scenarios encode the correct direction (SecurityError → NotAllowedError) — the `pay-no-activation` scenario expects `SecurityError (bug)` pre-135 and `NotAllowedError` 135+. Matches feature title and chromestatus.

interactive.rationale: Four real scenarios (payment × activation matrix) selectable by card, with a `run selected` and `run all` button, each issuing a real `credentials.create({publicKey, extensions: {payment}})` and rendering the live error.name in a log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb promises four scenarios with actual error name vs pre/135 expected — page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the 2×2 payment/activation matrix, but the `hasActivation: true` scenarios fire without a real click-triggered call (the button click IS the activation, but the test runner consumes it before the create runs, so all four scenarios effectively run with no activation). Authors won't see the success path.

references.rationale: No `see also` or `references` section anywhere on the page — required for the cleanup script to recover the slug. The chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link is missing entirely.

design_system.rationale: All colours via CSS variables and color-mix; scenario cards highlight active via accent-blue border; no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add a references / see-also section with the chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail:
Page has no references section. Add a `

see also

` block with the chromestatus.com/feature/5160752715137024 link, the W3C SPC spec, and links to the three sibling concepts. This is a hard requirement per CLAUDE.md — the slug-recovery script needs it.

openQuestions.title: Actually preserve user activation across scenarios
openQuestions.detail: The scenario card click is the user activation. By the time `runScenario` fires inside the click handler, activation is preserved — but `runAll` walks the array with awaits, so activation is consumed by the first call and lost for the rest. Either run each scenario from its own click, or use `navigator.userActivation.isActive` to verify before calling, and surface the diagnostic.

openQuestions.title: Reproduce in a cross-origin iframe
openQuestions.detail: Same issue as the siblings: the bug only manifested in a cross-origin iframe. From top-level both versions throw NotAllowedError without activation. To actually distinguish pre-135 from 135+, embed an iframe pointing at another origin (e.g. via data: URL with a `payment` Permissions-Policy) and run the create from inside.

openQuestions.title: Surface payment Permissions-Policy as a probe
openQuestions.detail: Payment credential creation requires the `payment` Permissions-Policy header for cross-origin iframes. Add a probe that reads `document.featurePolicy?.allowedFeatures()` or `document.permissionsPolicy` and shows whether the current document is allowed — the missing permission is one of the things that throws.

summary: The most accurate of the four concepts in this feature — direction matches the spec, the four-scenario matrix is exactly the right framing. But the page is missing a references section (a hard requirement), and `run all` silently drops user activation after the first call, so the activation column is always empty.
